steps.title: Use residual signs
steps.content: A point has an actual $y$-value greater than the predicted $y$-value when the point is above the line of best fit.

steps.title: Count the points above
steps.content: Counting the plotted points above the line gives $5$ points. The remaining $4$ plotted points are below the line.

steps.title: Match the count
steps.content: The number of actual $y$-values greater than their predicted values is $5$. The answer is C.
